name: Mongolian Beef with Steamed Rice
description: Tender beef in a savory-sweet soy and ginger sauce — better than takeout and done in 20 minutes.
recipeIngredient: 1.5 pounds flank steak or sirloin, thinly sliced against the grain
1 teaspoons fresh ginger, grated (or 1/2 tsp ground ginger)
recipeInstructions: Coat the beef: Toss 1.5 pounds flank steak or sirloin, thinly sliced against the grain in 0.3 cups cornstarch until lightly coated. Set aside while you make the sauce.

Make the sauce: In a small bowl, whisk together 0.5 cups low-sodium soy sauce, 0.5 cups brown sugar, packed, 0.5 cups water, 4 pieces garlic cloves, minced, 1 teaspoons fresh ginger, grated (or 1/2 tsp ground ginger), and 0.5 teaspoons sesame oil. Set aside.
Cook the rice: Cook 2 cups long-grain white rice according to package directions.

Sear the beef: Heat 2 tbsp of 3 tablespoons vegetable oil, divided in a large skillet or wok over high heat until nearly smoking. Add beef in a single layer (work in batches if needed). Cook 1–2 min per side until browned. Remove and set aside.

Simmer the sauce: Reduce heat to medium. Add remaining 1 tbsp 3 tablespoons vegetable oil, divided and pour in the sauce. Let it simmer 2–3 min until it thickens slightly. Return beef to the pan, toss to coat, and cook 1 more minute.
Serve: Serve over rice, topped with 3 pieces green onions, sliced and 2 teaspoons sesame seeds (optional).
